title: SHIP IT — Beating the Posting Blocker
tagline: The scripts are written. The board is queued. ~35 min on the only blocker that's about courage, not systems.
fin_text: Identity shift complete. You don't 'try to post' anymore — you're someone who publishes. Streak's alive; protect it.

levels.title: LEVEL 0 · NAME THE REAL BLOCKER
levels.html:
The evidence (it's not ability)

scripts-tomorrow.md: two videos fully scripted. content-board.md: queued for a '7:30am shoot.' The 5-day SSA flywheel: written end to end. T006/T007 (just press record): open for 10+ days. Your own OS already diagnosed it: 'insecurity, not ability.' Every system-level fix exists; what's left is the human moment.

Reframe: posting is a rep, not a verdict

You ship games to the public without flinching — because a game feels like a THING, and a post feels like YOU. Wrong frame. A post is a rep. Wordle's grid, your challenge links, the Maccabeats video — none were verdicts on their makers. Volume beats judgment: nobody remembers anyone's weak post; everybody remembers who shows up.

quiz.q: Why do you ship games fearlessly but freeze on posts?
opts: A game feels like a THING; a post feels like YOU — and that frame is wrong
quiz.ex: Same public, same internet, same you. The fear is the self-exposure frame, not the act. Rep-framing ('this is practice unit #4') is what dissolves it — and it's trainable.

mission.txt: Say it in the nucleus, in your words: 'The blocker is fear of being seen, not ability.' Named monsters shrink. (Claude saves it; it never has to be said again.)

levels.title: LEVEL 1 · SHRINK TO THE PASTE
levels.html:
The smallest possible action

Your system pre-decides everything decidable: caption written, platform chosen, time set. The ONLY human step left is paste + publish — under 2 minutes. If a posting task ever feels bigger than 2 minutes, it isn't shrunk enough yet: send it back to Claude for pre-staging.

Claude waits with you

It's in ssa-week-1.md already: the post moment happens DURING a session, not alone after one. Standup names the post → you paste → you say 'posted' → ✅ + the streak ticks. Accountability without an audience.

quiz.q: A posting task feels heavy. The rule says…
opts: It isn't shrunk enough — everything but paste+publish goes back to pre-staging
quiz.ex: Heaviness = undecided decisions hiding in the task. Captions, platform, timing, thumbnail — all pre-stageable. Only the 2-minute human moment is yours, by design.

mission.txt: Pick tomorrow's ONE post with Claude right now — caption finalized, platform locked, time set. Tomorrow you only paste.

levels.title: LEVEL 2 · THE UNLOSABLE GAME
levels.html:
Post the confession first

Your scripted 'Human Confession' is the perfect opener BECAUSE it's about not-posting. Publishing it converts the blocker itself into content — the fear becomes the proof you beat it. Maximum honesty, minimum pretense, and your ICP (people scared of selling/showing up) sees themselves in it.

The recovery rule (this is the genius part)

Miss a day? The next post is 'why I didn't post yesterday.' The game has no losing state: post = win, miss = material for a more honest post = win. Streak-breaking shame is the #1 quitter-maker, and this rule deletes it.

quiz.q: You missed yesterday. What's today's post?
opts: 'Why I didn't post yesterday' — the miss IS the material
quiz.ex: Unlosable game design: every state has a next move. Honesty-as-content also happens to be your strongest genre — the confession posts are the ones your audience shares.

mission.txt: PUBLISHING MISSION (double XP): record the Human Confession (T006) — phone, one take, no edit polish. Done is the bar; published is the goal. Tell Claude when it's UP.

levels.title: LEVEL 3 · THE STREAK + BOSS
levels.html:
Make publishing visible and addictive

Published = done lands on the board with a streak counter — your dopamine wiring, used FOR you (you built an XP academy in a day; you know exactly how well this works on you). Targets from the audit: 1 video this week · 5 posts by month-end · 20+ in month two.

The identity finish line

Around rep ~20 the frame flips from 'trying to post' to being someone who posts — and identity is self-maintaining where willpower isn't. Everything downstream (SSA flywheel, Miklat clips, the JTA pitch) is gated on this one habit. It's the highest-ROI skill in your whole portfolio.

opts: Fear of being seen — and it's trainable with reps

boss.q: The only human step in a properly staged post:

opts: Tomorrow's post: 'why I didn't post yesterday'
A reason to rebuild the system

boss.q: What's downstream of this one habit?
opts: The SSA flywheel, Miklat clips, press pitches — the whole growth engine

mission.txt: PUBLISHING MISSION (double XP): post it. The confession video goes UP on the chosen platform, today or at tomorrow's pre-set time. Then say the word to Claude: 'posted.' Streak: 1.
